title: Marketing-sourced deals close 13 days faster this week
anchor: Current week wins averaged 38 days to close versus 51 days across the prior 11 weeks.
connect: Seven of nine wins came from mid-market accounts running fintech workflows.
body: Current week wins averaged 38 days to close versus 51 days across the prior 11 weeks. Seven of nine wins came from mid-market accounts running fintech workflows.
trace: Deal velocity compression pattern visible in marketing-sourced pipeline, concentrated in mid-market segment.

title: Enterprise win rate climbs to 31% in Q2
anchor: Q2 enterprise deals close at 30.77% versus the trailing four-quarter average of 22%.
connect: Average won deal size sits at $187K versus the four-quarter baseline of $142K.
body: Q2 enterprise deals close at 30.77% versus the trailing four-quarter average of 22%. Average won deal size sits at $187K versus the four-quarter baseline of $142K.
trace: Enterprise segment showing improved conversion and ASP discipline simultaneously in Q2.

title: Event sourcing shows 25-day cycle compression
anchor: Q1 event-sourced wins closed at 45 days versus Q4's 71-day average.
connect: SaaS Connect generated all 20 Q1 event wins versus 15 total event wins in Q4.
body: Q1 event-sourced wins closed at 45 days versus Q4's 71-day average. SaaS Connect generated all 20 Q1 event wins versus 15 total event wins in Q4.
trace: Event channel velocity improvement concentrated entirely in SaaS Connect performance.

title: Mid-market SQL volume up 50% in April
anchor: April mid-market SQLs run at 147 month-to-date versus the Q1 monthly average of 98.
connect: Fintech accounts represent 10 of 12 recent mid-market closed wins.
body: April mid-market SQLs run at 147 month-to-date versus the Q1 monthly average of 98. Fintech accounts represent 10 of 12 recent mid-market closed wins.
trace: Mid-market SQL generation surge coinciding with fintech segment concentration in closed deals.

title: ABM acceptance rate runs 20 points higher
anchor: ABM MQLs convert to SDR acceptance at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM volume.
connect: ABM generated 70 MQLs in April versus 77 non-ABM MQLs.
body: ABM MQLs convert to SDR acceptance at 61% versus 42% for non-ABM volume. ABM generated 70 MQLs in April versus 77 non-ABM MQLs.
trace: ABM showing superior qualification rates with roughly equal volume contribution.

title: Paid social pipeline creation flips paid search
anchor: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search.
connect: Q1 ran the opposite: $720K paid search versus $410K paid social.
body: Q2 paid social generated $620K in pipeline versus $380K from paid search. Q1 ran the opposite: $720K paid search versus $410K paid social.
trace: Channel performance reversal between Q1 and Q2 in paid channels.

title: Digital ads budget carries $84K reallocation gap
anchor: Q2 digital spend sits at $256K versus the $340K plan, leaving $84K unspent.
connect: Two event line items freed up the gap: SaaS Connect and Signal Summit totaling $84K.
body: Q2 digital spend sits at $256K versus the $340K plan, leaving $84K unspent. Two event line items freed up the gap: SaaS Connect and Signal Summit totaling $84K.
trace: Event budget reallocation creating available digital spend capacity in Q2.

title: Branded search volume doubles over six weeks
anchor: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921, growing each of the past six weeks.
connect: Cumulative growth over the window sits at 92%.
body: Weekly branded search climbed from 1000 to 1921, growing each of the past six weeks. Cumulative growth over the window sits at 92%.
trace: Sustained branded search momentum with consistent week-over-week gains.

title: Atlas mentions lead Beacon by 630 in April
anchor: April brand mentions show Atlas at 1840 versus top competitor Beacon at 1210.
connect: Analyst mentions climbed to 9 in the past 14 days versus 7 in the prior 30.
body: April brand mentions show Atlas at 1840 versus top competitor Beacon at 1210. Analyst mentions climbed to 9 in the past 14 days versus 7 in the prior 30.
trace: Brand mention leadership coinciding with increased analyst attention frequency.

title: Direct traffic crosses organic for the first time
anchor: April traffic runs 42.5% direct versus 36.4% organic search.
connect: Direct first exceeded organic on a cumulative basis on April 15th.
body: April traffic runs 42.5% direct versus 36.4% organic search. Direct first exceeded organic on a cumulative basis on April 15th.
trace: Direct traffic milestone reached mid-April, indicating brand recognition strength.

title: Atlas Insights reference opt-in rate hits 76%
anchor: Atlas Insights customers opt into references at 76% versus Atlas Connect's 28%.
connect: Atlas Workflow sits between them at 71% opt-in rate.
body: Atlas Insights customers opt into references at 76% versus Atlas Connect's 28%. Atlas Workflow sits between them at 71% opt-in rate.
trace: Product-level reference willingness showing significant variation across Atlas suite.

title: Named accounts show 40% high-intent coverage

connect: The Named Account universe includes 50 target companies.
body: Two of three accounts with 5+ high-intent events sit on the Named Accounts list. The Named Account universe includes 50 target companies.

title: Atlas Assist launch calendar locks tomorrow

connect: This marks the next product launch in the Atlas suite expansion.
body: May 8th Atlas Assist launch shows campaign status ready with calendar locking April 24th. This marks the next product launch in the Atlas suite expansion.

title: SDR capacity runs 48% above inbound volume
anchor: Current week shows 210 SDR capacity versus 142 inbound leads.
connect: The trailing four weeks averaged 212 capacity versus 169 inbound volume.
body: Current week shows 210 SDR capacity versus 142 inbound leads. The trailing four weeks averaged 212 capacity versus 169 inbound volume.
trace: SDR capacity running consistently above inbound flow for the past month.

title: Forrester and G2 tie for analyst mention lead

connect: Gartner contributed 1 mention over the same period.
body: Past 14 days show Forrester and G2 each contributing 3 of 9 total analyst mentions. Gartner contributed 1 mention over the same period.

title: Snowflake plus dbt stack appears in all recent mid-market wins
anchor: Last 60 days show 12 mid-market closed-wins, all running Snowflake plus dbt configurations.
body: Last 60 days show 12 mid-market closed-wins, all running Snowflake plus dbt configurations. Fintech represents 10 of those 12 wins.
trace: Technology stack pattern showing perfect correlation in recent mid-market wins.
